title: Samsung Launches One UI 9 Beta for Galaxy S26 Series: AI Features, Security Upgrades, and Release Date
description: Samsung launches the One UI 9 beta for Galaxy S26 on Android 17, adding smarter Notes tools, a redesigned Quick Panel, unified TalkBack, and stronger app security. The stable rollout is expected in July 2026 with the Galaxy Z Fold 8 and Z Flip 8. The post Samsung Launches One UI 9 Beta for Galaxy S26 Series: AI Features, Security Upgrades, and Release Date appeared first on Memeburn .

title: AI navigation opens new pathways for triage and support
description: Valentia Technologies has announced the development of an advanced AI navigation and safety component for its digital front-door platform OctansCare, a feature designed to assist users’ safe and confident navigation of “complex health and mental health support pathways.” Built on advanced research in responsible AI, clinical safety, service navigation and human-centred design, the component adds [...]

title: World Telecom Day 2026: Resilient networks to drive India’s digital future in AI age, say executives
description: NEW DELHI: Amid a rapidly expanding digital economy in India, top industry executives said resilient and intelligent telecom networks are becoming crucial for financial systems, governance platforms, enterprises, and artificial intelligence (AI)-driven ecosystems. On the occasion of the World Telecommunication and Information Society Day (WTISD), 2026, the executives from telecom, satellite, digital services, and network infrastructure sectors highlighted that resilient communication networks will drive the next phase of India’s digital growth.

title: Lotes holds prices to gain share as server and AI connectors drive record revenue
description: Connector maker Lotes said it held product prices to win market share and reported record first-quarter 2026 revenue as strong server demand lifted results. The company disclosed that consolidated revenue for the first quarter reached NT$9.332 billion (US$295.12 million), gross margin was 49.55%, operating margin was 29.04%, net profit was NT$2.4 billion, and earnings per share were NT$21.72, with the server business accounting for more than 50% of total revenue for the first time.
